evidence_quotes.quote: Payment processors like Visa and Mastercard have recently pressured the digital distribution service \"Steam\" to take away certain games from their store even though they were perfectly legal.
This action was taken after the Australian group \"Collective Shout\" pressured the aforementioned payment processors.

I find it most troubling that this group; openly anti-abortion and anti-LGBTQ has a power to block the sale of legitimate products I feel very uncomfortable that payment processors can decide which products aren't available to me solely because they might contain inappropriate topics.
This is a risk for creative freedom and consumer access.
For now the adult content has been the target, tomorrow it could be any content that might be deemed inappropriate, such as stories regarding LGBTQ, mental health or political commentary.

Furthermore, I am also concerned about the issue of game preservation; Video-game companies have been \"killing\" their games; meaning that for any reason they can shut down the game's servers (Where the game virtually functions), making the product you paid for be non-functional, unusable.
There has been a very active \"Stop Killing Games\" campaign centered around this topic which has seen great support.
In order to protect our products from being censored, delisted or destroyed we need the Digital Fairness Act.

paragraphs.text: As an avid consumer, I believe digital fairness is very important. Here are some of my key points; Payment processors like Visa and Mastercard have recently pressured the digital distribution service "Steam" to take away certain games from their store even though they were perfectly legal. Under the guise that they contained inappropriate content. While far from tasteful, nothing illegal was depicted in these products. This action was taken after the Australian group "Collective Shout" pressured the aforementioned payment processors. I find it most troubling that this group; openly anti-abortion and anti-LGBTQ has a power to block the sale of legitimate products I feel very uncomfortable that payment processors can decide which products aren't available to me solely because they might contain inappropriate topics. I believe art should be allowed to tackle any subject. It sets a dangerous precedent going forward, as games which discuss/explore LGBTQ topics are in danger. This is a risk for creative freedom and consumer access. For now the adult content has been the target, tomorrow it could be any content that might be deemed inappropriate, such as stories regarding LGBTQ, mental health or political commentary. Furthermore, I am also concerned about the issue of game preservation; Video-game companies have been "killing" their games; meaning that for any reason they can shut down the game's servers (Where the game virtually functions), making the product you paid for be non-functional, unusable. I believe a product I bought with my money should not cease to function just due to the company's decision. There has been a very active "Stop Killing Games" campaign centered around this topic which has seen great support. In order to protect our products from being censored, delisted or destroyed we need the Digital Fairness Act.
